title: cacalia pedunculata
intro: Cacalia pedunculata, also known as Yellow Ragwort or Wild Cacalia, is a perennial plant species native to Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Cacalia pedunculata has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting digestive health. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
